title: FAQ 1 - Consequences of Unpaid Payroll Deductions
full_text: What are the immediate consequences of unpaid payroll deductions? Question, will the CRA freeze my bank accounts, garnish receivables, or seize assets right away? Not right away, but they will begin to play games such as contacting your bank, saying to your bank, please lock the account or please give us money. We've covered this before. The CRA does not have access to your bank account. It is the bank that gives them money after they ask for it. They will do that. Yes. And if they follow chapter 10 of the auditor's handbook, then they will call your wife, call your kids, call your staff, trespass all over your land until you give them a no trespassing notice. And they will contact your suppliers, which is why none of you should ever have your client list on your websites. And none of you should ever, ever, ever, ever collect GST or charge GST into the same corporation that you hold assets or client list within. everything corporation a has your assets and your client was corporation b is the one that does commerce corporation b has no assets like everyone's got to separate everything why do all of you have just one little corporation thinking it's going to protect you in some way it doesn't work that way that's okay though we're going to talk about corporations in a couple of weeks so they will they're not going to seize your assets what they will do is they will get um a writ, but what they always do to get a writ is they go to tax court, which does not have jurisdiction over equity. So if they do go to tax court to get a writ on your property, we file a motion in the superior court of the province where your property is and we get it thrown out because we invoke clause 7 of the taxpayers bill of rights which states you do not owe tax dollars whilst you are in dispute and then we go to the aboriginal international network which is a third-party tribunal which has government funding and government recognition as a legitimate tribunal and they subpoena the cra to come out and the cra never shows up so my clients always win then we take the tribunal victory and we go to court and we win the motion as well. It's a pain in the ass. You know the quickest way to not have these problems? Stop snitching on yourself, man. Why do you people keep doing your freaking tax returns? It's insane to me. The only reason any of you have tax problems is because you did tax returns in the first place. But enough of that. How quickly do they move on enforcement once arrears are identified? Oh, I don't know. We Most of our clients are like two, three years behind in their payroll. But I would say that by the time you do get into one full year of being behind in payroll, the harassing phone calls are going to start. The letters are going to come in. So if you are behind on payroll, we talked to a company this morning, really early too. I had to get up at four for this conversation because the guy was in Germany at the time. he has three million dollars of payroll that he has not paid to the CRA and his only solution is to sell the corporation to me and let me take that debt over from him and then the CRA can sue me which you know they won't do so it's possible for anybody to get behind the eight ball when you're dealing with payroll which is also why I have a payroll company and I'll cover that last I'm going to solve everybody's payroll problems in tonight's show
